commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is explicit security-owner approval and branch-protection rollout confirmation, not an autonomous code repair.; Needs attention: This intentionally changes a security-sensitive pull_request_target dependency gate, so secops/owner approval is required even though no concrete code-execution defect was found. (sha=7cd873ca74ed8e8bceef58e7dc89cffa29a60670)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow with a checked-in Node guard script, SHA-bound dependency-change approval handling, CODEOWNERS coverage, and script/workflow tests.

PR surface: Tests +290, Config -141, Other +630. Total +779 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a CI/security-gate feature PR, not a bug report with a current-main reproduction path. The relevant proof is workflow/script review plus the branch's focused validation commands and CI status.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is explicit security-owner approval and branch-protection rollout confirmation, not an autonomous code repair.

target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments for dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, and package manifest fields that can affect dependency resolution or install behavior.
- Hard-fails dependency graph changes unless an explicit `OPENCLAW_SECURITY_APPROVERS` allowlist member authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Preserves the guard comment as a freshness barrier when graph changes are removed, so old allow comments cannot authorize later reintroduced graph changes.

## Notes

- Script-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/dependency-change-awareness.yml scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
